_comment: dBASE III PLUS 1.1 .dbf byte normalization map.
and section 4 (32-byte field descriptor table). Byte-verified against six III+ 1.1
golden fixtures: BANK, CLIENTS, TOURS, TRAVEL, TAX, UNIVERSD.
III+ ONLY. Provenance: the corpus III+-only table, NOT docs/research/dbase-ground-truth.md
(which straddles III+/IV and marks 0x1C/0x1F as 'MEANINGFUL iff .MDX'). That brief
classification is superseded for III+ work by ADR-0008 DEC-06.
MEANINGFUL = bytes whose value carries semantics for III+ round-trip correctness.
A byte-exact reader/writer MUST preserve or reproduce these bytes.
NORMALIZE = bytes that are always 0x00 in 100% of III+ fixtures and whose
stated IV/VFP meaning does NOT apply to III+. Normalize to 0x00 on write;
do not error on nonzero, but do not propagate IV meaning.
status: III+ period-grounded; byte-verified against six III+ 1.1 golden fixtures

header_bytes.name: version
header_bytes.note: 0x03 or 0x83 in III+; bit 7 = has memo. Ref: dbf.md ss3.

header_bytes.name: last_update_year
header_bytes.note: year - 1900. Part of last-update date (YY,MM,DD). Ref: dbf.md ss2.

header_bytes.name: nrec
header_bytes.note: record count, u32 LE. Authoritative for reading. Ref: dbf.md ss2, ss8.

header_bytes.name: header_length
header_bytes.note: byte offset of first record, u16 LE. Trust this. Ref: dbf.md ss4, ss8.

header_bytes.name: record_length
header_bytes.note: bytes per record, u16 LE. 1 + sum(field_lengths). Ref: dbf.md ss6, ss8.

header_bytes.name: reserved_0x0C
header_bytes.note: 0x00 0x00 in all III+ fixtures. IV meaning not applicable. Ref: dbf.md ss2.

header_bytes.name: reserved_0x0E
header_bytes.note: 0x00 in III+ fixtures. IV: incomplete-transaction flag; not used in III+. Ref: dbf.md ss2.

header_bytes.name: reserved_0x0F
header_bytes.note: 0x00 in III+ fixtures. IV: encryption flag; not used in III+. Ref: dbf.md ss2.

header_bytes.name: reserved_multiuser_0x10
header_bytes.note: all 0x00 in III+ fixtures. Reserved for multi-user/LAN. Ref: dbf.md ss2.

header_bytes.name: mdx_flag
header_bytes.note: 0x00 in 100% of III+ fixtures. III+ has no MDX. IV production-.MDX flag meaning does NOT apply to III+. ADR-0008 DEC-06: NORMALIZE for III+. Ref: dbf.md ss2.

header_bytes.name: ldid
header_bytes.note: language driver / code-page id. 0x00 in all III+ 1.1 fixtures. A byte-exact III+ writer emits 0x00. Ref: dbf.md ss7.

header_bytes.name: reserved_0x1E
header_bytes.note: 0x00 0x00 in III+ fixtures. Ref: dbf.md ss2.

descriptor_bytes.name: field_name
descriptor_bytes.note: NUL-terminated ASCII, NUL-padded. Read to first NUL; trailing bytes may be garbage (stale). Ref: dbf.md ss4.

descriptor_bytes.name: field_type
descriptor_bytes.note: ASCII char: C N D L M for III+. Ref: dbf.md ss4, ss5.

descriptor_bytes.name: field_data_addr
descriptor_bytes.note: RAM pointer; meaningless on disk. Ignore on read; writer may emit anything. Normalize to 0x00000000 for deterministic output. Ref: dbf.md ss4 (0x0C row), ss9.

descriptor_bytes.name: field_length
descriptor_bytes.note: bytes the field occupies in each record. For N: includes sign + decimal point. Ref: dbf.md ss4.

descriptor_bytes.name: decimal_count
descriptor_bytes.note: digits after decimal point; meaningful for N only (0 for C/D/L/M). Ref: dbf.md ss4.

descriptor_bytes.name: reserved_desc_0x12
descriptor_bytes.note: 0x00 0x00 in fixtures. Reserved multi-user/LAN. Ref: dbf.md ss4.

descriptor_bytes.name: work_area_id
descriptor_bytes.note: 0x00 or 0x01 in III+ fixtures. Do NOT import the Harbour/IV auto-increment meaning. Normalize to 0x00 for deterministic output. Ref: dbf.md ss4 (0x14 row), ss9.

descriptor_bytes.name: reserved_desc_0x15
descriptor_bytes.note: 0x00 0x00 in fixtures. Reserved multi-user/LAN. Ref: dbf.md ss4.

descriptor_bytes.name: set_fields_flag
descriptor_bytes.note: 0x00 in all III+ fixtures. Ref: dbf.md ss4 (0x17 row).

descriptor_bytes.name: reserved_desc_0x18
descriptor_bytes.note: 0x00 in fixtures. Reserved. Ref: dbf.md ss4 (0x18 row).

descriptor_bytes.name: index_field_flag
descriptor_bytes.note: 0x00 in 100% of III+ fixtures. IV: index-field / MDX-field flag; does NOT apply to III+. ADR-0008 DEC-06: NORMALIZE for III+. Ref: dbf.md ss4 (0x1F row).

record_bytes.name: field_data
record_bytes.note: fixed-width packed ASCII field data; no padding between fields. All types C/N/D/L/M are ASCII in III+. Ref: dbf.md ss5, ss6.
